notes: DDR5-6400 remained anchored by 64GB and 128GB over the last 7-14 days, with 96GB repaired but 32GB still lacking a stronger fresh exact public anchor.
DDR5-5600 stayed structurally split at 32GB across low-retail and high-distributor bands; this is still outlier_only rather than no_public_sample.
PCIe4 TLC remained the cleanest and most executable category, with the 30.72TB public band holding after its earlier recovery.
PCIe5 TLC nominal coverage remained acceptable, but 30.72TB still failed to recover the older lower-band anchor cleanly and continues to show stale/family split behaviour.
PCIe4 QLC stayed fully covered, while supplementation for high-capacity slots still leaned on parser-gap and anti-bot classification at Mouser, PCNation, and some Newegg direct pages.

noDirectSampleSources: 16GB exact comparable part still not recovered
16GB exact comparable part still not recovered
outlierOnlySources: A-Tech/Newegg/CDW Kingston low cluster vs Provantage/CDW/Connection high cluster for 32GB

noDirectSampleSources: Provantage/SHI lower-band exact 30.72TB public anchor still missing
outlierOnlySources: historical AAAWave sold-out low cluster for 30.72TB

keyChangesVsYesterday: No tracked representative value changed versus 2026-04-22; the market still looks like stable main bands plus unresolved structural splits.
ComputerDealsDirect's old DDR5-6400 128GB direct page now returns 404, which weakens that legacy supplementation path without yet invalidating the tracked representative value.
AAAWave's old PCIe5 TLC 30.72TB direct page now returns 404 and A5 IT search no longer gives a cleaner replacement anchor, so the slot remains stale_repeated_sample rather than recovering.

Mouser again returned denied content and PCNation again returned Cloudflare gating, which further confirms parser_gap for PCIe4 QLC high-capacity supplementation rather than true no-public-sample absence.

DDR5-6400.reason: 64GB and 128GB remain stable, 96GB remains visible after the repair, but 32GB still lacks a stronger clean exact anchor and 16GB is still absent.

DDR5-5600.reason: 32GB remains structurally split across low retail and high distributor bands, while 64GB and 128GB remain visible but not at attractive low levels.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: This remains the most stable and fully covered category, with repeatable public distribution bands and no new sign of anchor decay.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: Lower capacities remain observable, but 30.72TB still lacks a trustworthy single representative public band because the old lower anchor remains stale while cleaner replacements did not materialize today.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: Main premium public bands remain usable, but high-capacity low clusters and parser-gated supplementation remain too noisy to justify a one-shot bottom-call.

gaps.note: Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.
Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.
Low-side public pages remain far below the higher distributor band, and today's checks still did not produce a unified representative public band.
The previously preferred lower-band anchor still did not repeat cleanly; today even legacy supplementation pages degraded further instead of recovering.
The pages again returned denied or Cloudflare-gated content, confirming access/parser failure rather than true absence of public market pricing.
